title: Interpret exponential expressions word problems
description: Interpret the meaning of the coefficients of exponential functions in terms of the real-world relationship they model.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $5(2)^t$ gives the number of leaves on a plant as a function of the number of weeks since it was planted.

**What does $2$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Weeks since the plant was planted $(t)$ | Number of leaves on the plant $(5\cdot2^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $5\cdot2^0=5$
$1$ | $5\cdot2^1=10$
$2$ | $5\cdot2^2=20$
$3$ | $5\cdot2^3=40$
We see that each week, the number of leaves increases by a factor of $2$.

**The number of leaves is multiplied by $2$ each week.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: The plant was planted $2$ weeks ago.
There were initially $2$ leaves on the plant.
The number of leaves is multiplied by $2$ each week.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $6.7\cdot1.45^t$ models the number of billions of genetic sequence bases available in the public database of the Whole Genome Shotgun project $t$ years since $2002$.

**What does $1.45$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since $2002$ $(t)$ | Billions of genetic sequence bases in the database $(6.7\cdot1.45^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $6.7\cdot1.45^0=6.7$
$1$ | $6.7\cdot1.45^1=9.715$
$2$ | $6.7\cdot1.45^2\approx 14$
$3$ | $6.7\cdot1.45^3\approx 20.4$
We see that each year, the number of genetic sequence bases increases by a factor of $1.45$.

**The number of bases in the database is multiplied by $1.45$ each year.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: It is $1.45$ years since $2002$
There were $1.45$ billion bases in the database in $2002$.
The number of bases in the database is multiplied by $1.45$ each year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $11(1.022)^t$ models the per capita gross domestic product (GDP) of the US, in thousands of dollars, as a function of the number of years since $1950$.

**What does $1.022$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since $1950$ $(t)$ | Per capita GDP, in thousands of dollars, $(11\cdot1.022^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $11\cdot1.022^0=11$
$1$ | $11\cdot1.022^1=11.242$
$2$ | $11\cdot1.022^2\approx 11.489$
$3$ | $11\cdot1.022^3\approx 11.742$
We see that each year, the per capita GDP increases by a factor of $1.022$.

**The per capita GDP in the US is multiplied by approximately $1.022$ each year.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: The maximum per capita GDP occurred $1.022$ years after $1950$.
The per capita GDP in the US was $\$1022$ in $1950$.
The per capita GDP in the US is multiplied by approximately $1.022$ each year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $1350(1.05)^t$ models the average wages, in dollars, in the US as a function of the number of years since $1930$.

**What does $1.05$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since $1930$ $(t)$ | Average wages, in dollars, $(1350\cdot1.05^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $1350\cdot1.05^0=1350$
$1$ | $1350\cdot1.05^1=1417.50$
$2$ | $1350\cdot1.05^2\approx 1488.38$
$3$ | $1350\cdot1.05^3\approx 1562.79$
We see that each year, the average wages increase by a factor of $1.05$, which means they increase by $5\%$.

**The average wages in the US increase by about $5\%$ each year.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: The average wages double every $1.05$ years.
The average wages in the US were about $\$1.05$ in $1930$.
The average wages in the US increase by about $5\%$ each year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $475\cdot1.076^t$ models the average annual per capita health care costs, in dollars, in the US as a function of the number of years since $1970$.

[http://www.statisticbrain.com/health-insurance-cost-statistics/]: (Source)

**What does $1.076$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since $1970$ $(t)$ | Per capita health care costs, in dollars, $(475\cdot1.076^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $475\cdot1.076^0=475$
$1$ | $475\cdot1.076^1=511.10$
$2$ | $475\cdot1.076^2\approx 549.94$
$3$ | $475\cdot1.076^3\approx 591.74$
We see that each year, the average per capita health care costs increase by a factor of $1.076$, which means they increase by $7.6\%$.

**The per capita health care costs increase by about $7.6\%$ each year.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: The per capita health care costs double every $76$ years.
The per capita health care costs were about $\$1{,}076$ in $1970$.
The per capita health care costs increase by about $7.6\%$ each year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $1.5\cdot1.09^t$ models the housing costs, in thousands of dollars, for summer term $t$ years since Chinedu applied to his college.

[http://www.statisticbrain.com/health-insurance-cost-statistics/]: (Source)

**What does $1.09$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since Chinedu applied to his college $(t)$ | Summer housing costs, in thousands of dollars, $(1.5\cdot1.09^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $1.5\cdot1.09^0=1.5$
$1$ | $1.5\cdot1.09^1=1.635$
$2$ | $1.5\cdot1.09^2\approx 1.782$
$3$ | $1.5\cdot1.09^3\approx 1.943$
We see that each year, the summer housing costs increase by a factor of $1.09$, which means they increase by $9\%$.

**The summer housing costs increase by $9\%$ each year.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: Chinedu applied to his school $9$ years ago.
The summer housing costs were $\$1{,}090$ the year that Chinedu applied to his college.
The summer housing costs increase by $9\%$ each year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $3(1.5)^t$ models the number of bacteria in a culture as a function of the number of hours since the culture was created.

**What does $3$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Hours since the culture was created $(t)$ | Number of bacteria in the culture $(3(1.5)^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $3(1.5)^0=3$
$1$ | $3(1.5)^1=4.5$
$2$ | $3(1.5)^2=6.75$
$3$ | $3(1.5)^3=10.125$
We see that $0$ hours after the culture was created, there were $3$ bacteria in the culture.

**There were initially $3$ bacteria in the culture.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: The culture was created $3$ hours ago.
There were initially $3$ bacteria in the culture.
The number of bacteria is multiplied by $3$ each hour.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $900\cdot2.25^t$ gives the price performance, in bits per second per dollar, of wireless devices from a certain company as a function of years since the company began.

**What does $900$ represent in this expression?**

[http://singularity.com/charts/page77.html]: (Based on this source)

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since the company began $(t)$ | Price efficiency in bits per second per dollar $(900(2.25)^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $900(2.25)^0=900$
$1$ | $900(2.25)^1=2{,}025$
$2$ | $900(2.25)^2=4{,}556.25$
$3$ | $900(2.25)^3=10{,}251.5625$
We see that $0$ years after the company began, the company had a price efficiency of $900$ bits per second per dollar.

**The company's initial price efficiency was $900$ bits per second per dollar.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: The company will have its maximum price efficiency $900$ years after it began.
The company's initial price efficiency was $900$ bits per second per dollar.
The price efficiency is multiplied by $900$ each year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $60\cdot1.2^t$ models the number of nano-related patents granted in the US as a function of years since $1991$.

**What does $60$ represent in this expression?**

[http://singularity.com/charts/page84.html]: (Based on this source)

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since $1991$ $(t)$ | Number of nano-related patents granted $(60\cdot1.2^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $60\cdot1.2^0=60$
$1$ | $60\cdot1.2^1=72$
$2$ | $60\cdot1.2^2=86.4$
$3$ | $60\cdot1.2^3=103.68$
We see that $0$ years after $1991$, the US granted $60$ nano-related patents.

**There were $60$ nano-related patents granted in $1991$.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: The first nano-related patents were granted $60$ years before $1991$.
There were $60$ nano-related patents granted in $1991$.
The number of nano-related patents granted is multiplied by $60$ each year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $5\cdot 1.15^t$ gives the area, in square meters, damaged by a fire as a function of minutes since the fire was discovered.

**What does $5$ represent in this expression?**

[http://www.iafss.org/publications/fss/1/657/view]: (Based on this source)

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Minutes since the fire was discovered $(t)$ | Area, in square meters, damaged by the fire $(5\cdot1.15^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $5\cdot1.15^0=5$
$1$ | $5\cdot1.15^1=5.75$
$2$ | $5\cdot1.15^2\approx6.61$
$3$ | $5\cdot1.15^3\approx7.60$
We see that $0$ minutes after the fire was discovered, there was an area of $5$ square meters damaged by the fire.

**An area of $5$ square meters had been damaged by the fire by the time it was discovered.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: It has been $5$ minutes since the fire was discovered.
An area of $5$ square meters had been damaged by the fire by the time it was discovered.
The area damaged by the fire is multiplied by $5$ each minute.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $4\cdot3^t$ gives the number of ducks living in Anoop's pond $t$ years after he built it.

**What does $4$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since Anoop built the pond $(t)$ |  Number of ducks living in the pond $(4\cdot3^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $4\cdot3^0=4$
$1$ | $4\cdot3^1=12$
$2$ | $4\cdot3^2=36$
$3$ | $4\cdot3^3=108$
We see that $0$ years after Anoop built the pond, there were $4$ ducks living there.

**There were $4$ ducks living in Anoop's pond when he first built it.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: It has been $4$ years since Anoop built the pond.
There were $4$ ducks living in Anoop's pond when he first built it.
The number of ducks living in Anoop's pond is multiplied by $4$ every year.

assessment_items.question_content:
The expression $1.01\cdot1.005^t$ gives the amount of money, in thousands of dollars, in Carter's savings account $t$ years after he opens it.

**What does $1.01$ represent in this expression?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's explore this model using a table.

Years since Carter opened the account $(t)$ |  Amount of money, in thousands of dollars, in the account $(1.01\cdot1.005^t)$
-|-
$0$ | $1.01\cdot1.005^0=1.01$
$1$ | $1.01\cdot1.005^1=1.01505$
$2$ | $1.01\cdot1.005^2\approx1.02013$
$3$ | $1.01\cdot1.005^3\approx 1.02523$
We see that $0$ years after Carter opened the account, there was a total of $1.01$ thousand dollars in the account. That is the same as saying the account had $\$1{,}010$.

**Carter's savings account had $\$1{,}010$ in it when he opened it.**

In general, the expression $a\cdot b^x$ represents a context whose initial value (i.e. when $x=0$) is $a$ and where each time $x$ increases by $1$, the expression is multiplied by $b$.

choices.content: Carter opened the savings account $1$ year ago.
Carter's savings account had $\$1{,}010$ in it when he opened it.
The amount of money in Carter's savings account increase by $1\%$ each year.
